skill.name: general-video
skill.description: Author custom HyperFrames video compositions when no specialized workflow fits the request. It guides discovery, visual direction, layout, animation, audio, validation, and handoff.
skill.summary: Create custom HyperFrames videos with structured creative, layout, animation, audio, and validation guidance.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak cryptography findings, but review found these are mostly Markdown backticks, prose, CSS examples, and workflow references in SKILL.md. The skill is not blocked, but it does instruct agents to run local Node and npx commands, so publication should include an external-command warning.

medium_findings.title: External Command Workflow Requires Caution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run a local media engine with node and HyperFrames validation commands with npx. These commands are legitimate for video production, but they execute code from the local toolchain and installed packages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly shown in Markdown command examples and the output checklist. They are fixed workflow commands, not obfuscated malware, but they still require execution of local and package-managed tooling.

medium_findings.title: Delegated Audio Engine May Use Credentials Or External Services
medium_findings.description: The audio workflow delegates text to speech, music, and sound effects to a shared media engine. The documentation says behavior changes when HeyGen credentials are present and may use other generation providers when absent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill file does not contain the media engine code, so direct exfiltration is not proven. The documented workflow clearly depends on credentials and provider-backed audio generation, which merits a user warning.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary prose such as description, design, and style guidance, plus color and workflow text. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm implementation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown frontmatter and design workflow prose. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Static Findings Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts are caused by backticked skill names, filenames, CSS properties, and Markdown examples. These are documentation tokens, not Ruby shell execution syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and the cited spans use inline code formatting and tables. No Ruby source file or executable Ruby context exists in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command examples for node and npx. They are expected operational commands, but marketplace users should review installed tooling before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in shell-style Markdown snippets and checklists. They are not hidden, dynamic, or user-input concatenated commands.

content.value_statement: General video requests often need structure before animation begins. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through custom HyperFrames planning, production, audio, checks, and handoff.
actual_capabilities: Routes general video requests when specialized HyperFrames workflows do not fit.
Guides discovery for audience, platform, priorities, and variation needs.
Defines visual identity, concept angle, typography, density, and layout gates.
Plans scene structure, rhythm, timing, layout, animation, audio, and captions.
Provides references for title cards, data scenes, UI demos, transitions, and sub-compositions.
Requires lint, validation, inspect, contrast review, and preview handoff checks.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not a standalone video renderer.
It depends on sibling HyperFrames skills and installed HyperFrames tooling.
It does not support live at-render-time data or NLE-style editing of finished videos.
Audio generation may require credentials, provider access, or local media engine support.

use_cases.title: Produce a Brand Reel
use_cases.description: Create a multi-scene brand or sizzle reel when the request does not match a narrower product workflow.

use_cases.title: Build a Title Sequence
use_cases.description: Develop a polished title card, logo reveal, or lower-third with layout and motion guidance.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Video Concept
use_cases.description: Turn a vague video idea into a structured HyperFrames composition plan with validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Title Card
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short custom composition.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the general-video skill to create a 6-second HyperFrames title card for [brand or topic]. Keep it focused and do not add extra scenes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Scene Reel
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a longer piece.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the general-video workflow to plan and build a [duration] brand reel for [audience]. Include visual identity, scene structure, rhythm, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add Audio and Captions
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with narration or music.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use general-video to add narration, background music, and captions to this HyperFrames concept. Create the audio request only when audio is required.

prompt_templates.title: Direct a Complex Composition
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with several motion styles.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use general-video as the fallback workflow for a custom composition with typography, data moments, UI motion, transitions, and final HyperFrames validation.

output_examples.input: Create a launch-style video for an internal tool without a product page.
output_examples.output: A structured video plan with audience, concept angle, visual identity, scene sequence, animation references, audio needs, and validation checklist.

output_examples.input: Make a short animated title card for a conference session.
output_examples.output: A focused single-scene composition plan with typography, layout, foreground details, motion timing, and preview handoff requirements.

output_examples.input: Turn a loose brand mood into a 20-second visual montage.
output_examples.output: A multi-scene direction with pacing, transitions, density rules, media notes, caption guidance, and final inspection steps.

best_practices: Use this skill only when no specialized HyperFrames workflow clearly fits.
Ground visual choices in a design spec or the referenced creative guides before building.
Run the documented HyperFrames checks before delivering the final preview.
anti_patterns: Do not add extra scenes, music, or captions when the user asked for a small edit.
Do not skip layout validation and rely on animation to hide placement problems.
Do not run media generation commands without reviewing inputs, credentials, and output paths.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it for custom HyperFrames video work when a narrower workflow does not fit the input.

faq.question: Does it create the video by itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides an AI agent through planning, authoring, validation, and handoff in a HyperFrames project.

faq.question: Can it handle single-scene work?
faq.answer: Yes. It tells the agent to skip broad planning for title cards, small edits, and other focused requests.

faq.question: Does it support audio?
faq.answer: Yes, when the requested piece needs narration, music, sound effects, or captions through the shared media engine.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The workflow includes local node and npx commands. Users should review installed tools and command inputs before execution.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning for external command use and delegated media service behavior.
